lede: A look at September 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Stanisław Lem and Irène Joliot-Curie, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Typhoon Maemi
events.desc: Typhoon Maemi, the strongest recorded typhoon to strike South Korea, made landfall near Busan.

events.title: United Nations
events.desc: The United Nations lifts sanctions against Libya after that country agreed to accept responsibility and recompense the families of victims in the 1988 bombing of Pan Am Flight 103.

events.title: Soviet Union
events.desc: The USSR vetoes a United Nations Security Council Resolution deploring the Soviet destruction of Korean Air Lines Flight 007.

events.title: John F. Kennedy
events.desc: US President John F. Kennedy delivers his "We choose to go to the Moon" speech at Rice University.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: RMS Laconia, carrying civilians, Allied soldiers and Italian POWs is torpedoed off the coast of West Africa and sinks with a heavy loss of life.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler demands autonomy and self-determination for the Germans of the Sudetenland region of Czechoslovakia.

events.title: Leo Szilard
events.desc: Leó Szilárd, waiting for a red light on Southampton Row in Bloomsbury, conceives the idea of the nuclear chain reaction.

events.title: Gustav Mahler
events.desc: Premiere performance of Gustav Mahler's Symphony No. 8 in Munich (with a chorus of 852 singers and an orchestra of 171 players. Mahler's rehearsal assistant conductor was Bruno Walter).

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.desc: Mexican–American War: the Battle of Chapultepec begins.

births.name: Irène Joliot-Curie
births.line: French chemist and physicist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Louis C.K.
births.line: American comedian, actor, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Johnny Cash, American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and actor
Raja Aziz Bhatti, Pakistani Major who received the Nishan-e-Haider in the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965.
George Reid, Australian accountant and politician, 4th Prime Minister of Australia
Jacob Cats, Dutch poet, jurist, and politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ailbe (Elvis, Eilfyw) of Emly
Christian feast day: The Most Holy Name of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Christian feast day: John Henry Hobart (Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: Laisrén mac Nad Froích
